title: Protect shared mutable state with actors
description: Move mutable state that multiple tasks touch into an actor so the compiler serializes access instead of relying on manual locks.
body: Use when shared mutable state is accessed from concurrent Swift tasks. Do not guard it with ad-hoc DispatchQueue locks when an actor expresses the invariant, and do not capture non-Sendable state into a @Sendable closure. Actions: declare the state-owning type as `actor`, access its members with await from outside, keep synchronous invariants inside actor methods, and mark cross-task value types Sendable. Validation signals include the compiler's strict-concurrency checking (-strict-concurrency=complete) reporting no data-race warnings and Thread Sanitizer staying clean. Risk boundary: actor reentrancy means state can change across an await inside an actor method; re-check invariants after each await and escalate if a lock-step guarantee is actually required rather than assuming serialization implies atomic spans.
